headlineSummary: OpenAI drew the clearest negative sentiment on business strategy and lock-in concerns, while Anthropic and Google Gemini had mixed reactions and Microsoft Copilot was only lightly discussed; the neutral cluster is a close tie [E2][E3].

ranking.summary: OpenAI was the most negative target here, with comments warning about token-driven lock-in and a potential rug pull, though one note still credited it with helping accelerate development [E1][E2][E3].
Google Gemini had limited evidence and mixed sentiment, with one comment crediting it for speeding up development and another criticizing the provider’s loss-leading market strategy [E1][E2].

Anthropic drew mixed reactions: one comment praised it as acting responsibly, while another criticized its pricing as far more expensive, so the evidence balances out with no clear directional lean [E3][E4].
Microsoft Copilot had limited evidence and a mostly neutral signal, with one comment noting subsidy issues but also saying the product is being adjusted to remedy them [E3].

evidence.excerpt: I used Claude, Gemini, and ChatGPT heavily during bring-up for driver debugging, DT syntax, and kernel configuration issues. They accelerated development significantly, but the actual reverse engineering still required hands-on embedded Linux work: boot-chain analysis, DT bindings, panel timings, register experimentation, and kernel panic debugging.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Claude and says it 'accelerated development significantly,' which is mild praise despite the caveat that hands-on work was still required.
The comment explicitly names Gemini and credits it with significantly accelerating development, a clear positive assessment of usefulness.
The comment explicitly names ChatGPT and says it 'accelerated development significantly,' indicating mild praise for OpenAI's tool.

evidence.excerpt: Claude, OpenAI, Gemini et al. are quite literally burning through 100's of billions of dollars and selling it back to you for pennies on the dollar in the hopes that they get to be the only one left. If I spend $10 growing Oranges and sell them to you for $1; then of course it's more expensive for you to do the growing. I feel like I'm taking crazy pills. These models will become more expensive over time, it's functionally impossible for them not to, they just want to capture the market before they have to stop selling at a huge loss.

annotations.rationale: Using Claude as the Anthropic alias, the commenter criticizes the provider as pursuing loss-making market capture and unsustainable pricing.
The excerpt explicitly names OpenAI and criticizes it for burning huge sums and using underpriced access to capture the market.
The excerpt explicitly names Gemini and portrays its provider as selling at a huge loss to lock in the market, which is negative business-strategy sentiment.

evidence.excerpt: Github Copilot has been doing this with business and enterprise seats, but that will be coming to a head very soon. I expect a fast follow after june when they re-align consumer pro and pro+ accounts. OpenAi seems to be trying to throw tokens at clients to get lock in. So i'd be most worried about the rug pull that will come from open AI post IPO. Anthropic is already acting responsibly in this area and github copilot is attempting to remediate their insane subsidies in the next several months.

annotations.rationale: The comment says GitHub Copilot had 'insane subsidies' but is 'attempting to remediate' them, yielding mixed sentiment overall.
The commenter says OpenAI is 'trying to throw tokens at clients to get lock in' and warns of a coming 'rug pull,' which is strong criticism.
The excerpt explicitly says 'Anthropic is already acting responsibly in this area,' a direct mild endorsement of its pricing/business behavior.

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic is $15 per million output token which is 30-35x more expensive even in openrouter .

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly singles out Anthropic as '30-35x more expensive,' which is a straightforward pricing criticism.
